    Protocols / CEP

    Cognitive Efficiency Protocol.

    CEP scores every AI response on a 0–100 scale across four dimensions. It teaches the AI to act first, report concisely, and adapt its verbosity to the complexity of the task at hand.

    Get Started All Protocols

    What It Does

    An instruction protocol that optimizes AI output.

    CEP is injected via the system prompt. It does not touch the data flowing into the AI — that is the MCP server's job. Instead, CEP optimizes the AI's output tokens: how it communicates back. Four scoring dimensions are evaluated independently and combined into a single 0–100 score.

    01

    System Prompt Injection

    CEP instructions are injected automatically via lean-ctx's MCP server. No manual setup required.

    02

    Output Optimization

    Targets the AI's response tokens — not input. The AI learns to communicate more efficiently.

    03

    Four Dimensions

    Cache hit rate, read mode diversity, compression ratio, and action-first communication are scored independently.

    04

    Composite Score

    Dimensions combine into a 0–100 score. Higher means the AI is wasting fewer tokens communicating.

    Scoring

    The four dimensions.

    Each dimension measures a distinct aspect of communication efficiency. They are scored independently, then combined into the final CEP score.

    Cache

    How often the AI reuses cached file reads instead of re-reading the same file. Higher cache hit rates mean fewer redundant tokens flowing through the context window.

    Higher = better

    Modes

    How well the AI picks the right read mode for each task. Using map for context-only files and full for editable files shows efficient mode selection.

    Diverse = better

    Compress

    The ratio of compressed vs raw tokens in the context. Measures how effectively the AI leverages lean-ctx's compression modes rather than requesting uncompressed data.

    Higher ratio = better

    Action

    Whether the AI acts first and reports concisely, or narrates its plan before acting. Action-first communication eliminates wasted preamble tokens.

    Act first = better

    Adaptive Complexity

    Three levels. One protocol.

    CEP adapts its instruction set based on task complexity. Simple tasks get minimal instructions for maximum speed. Complex tasks get full analysis with quality anchoring.

    Mechanical

    Simple fixes, direct execution. No reasoning overhead needed — just do it.

    ACT1 · DELTA · 1LINE

    Standard

    Multi-file changes, reasoning needed. Brief explanation before acting.

    BRIEF · DELTA · NOREPEAT · STRUCT

    Architectural

    Design decisions, full analysis. Edge cases and trade-offs must be considered.

    FULL · QUALITY · NOREPEAT · STRUCT · FREF

    Reference

    15 instruction codes.

    Each code is a single keyword that tells the AI how to behave. CEP combines them based on task complexity. They can also be used manually in your prompts.

    Code Description
    ACT1 Act immediately, report result in one line
    BRIEF Summarize approach in 1–2 lines, then act
    FULL Outline approach, consider edge cases, then act
    DELTA Only show changed lines, not full files
    NOREPEAT Never repeat known context. Reference cached files by Fn ID
    STRUCT Use notation, not sentences. Changes: +line / -line / ~line
    1LINE One line per action. Summarize, don't explain
    NODOC Don't add comments that narrate what code does
    ACTFIRST Execute tool calls immediately. Never narrate before acting
    QUALITY Never skip edge case analysis or error handling to save tokens
    NOMOCK Never use mock data, fake values, or placeholder code
    FREF Reference files by Fn refs only, never full paths
    DIFF For code changes: show only diff lines, not full files
    ABBREV Use abbreviations: fn, cfg, impl, deps, req, res, ctx, err
    SYMBOLS Use TDD symbols: ⊕=add ⊖=remove ∆=modify →=returns ✓=ok ✗=fail

    Core Rules

    Five communication rules.

    These rules govern how the AI structures every response. They are non-negotiable — CEP enforces them regardless of complexity level.

    1

    ACT FIRST

    Execute tool calls immediately. Never narrate before acting. The AI should show results, not announce intentions.

    2

    DELTA ONLY

    Never repeat known context. Reference cached files by Fn ID. If the AI already read a file, it should reference F3:42, not quote the content again.

    3

    STRUCTURED OVER PROSE

    Use notation, not sentences. Changes: +line / -line / ~line (modified). Status: tool(args) → result.

    4

    ONE LINE PER ACTION

    Summarize, don't explain. Every action gets one line of output. No multi-paragraph descriptions of what was done.

    5

    QUALITY ANCHOR

    Never skip edge case analysis or error handling to save tokens. Complex tasks require full reasoning. Only reduce prose, never reduce thinking.

    Live Output

    What a CEP score looks like.

    CEP scores are appended to tool responses automatically. The AI sees its own efficiency metrics and adjusts behavior accordingly.

    CEP Score 
    CEP Score: 87/100

Dimensions:
  Cache hits      ████████░░ 82%
  Mode diversity  █████████░ 91%
  Compression     ████████░░ 85%
  Action-first    █████████░ 90%

Task complexity: Standard
Quality score:  >95% preservation

Instruction: BRIEF — Summarize approach
in 1–2 lines, then act.

    CEP activates automatically.

    Install lean-ctx, run init, and CEP starts scoring from the first interaction. No configuration, no flags, no env vars. It just works.

    Get Started All Protocols
